title: WSL2 on W10, CreateFile \\.\pipe\docker_engine: All pipe instances are busy.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Install of podman  v.4.1.1 on WSL2-W10 according to: [Podman for windows](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/podman-for-windows.md) fails.
First step `podman machine init` works, but not `podman machine start`:

```sh
podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        CreateFile \\.\pipe\docker_engine: All pipe instances are busy.

Podman clients are still able to connect.
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install [podman-v4.1.1.msi](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v4.1.1/podman-v4.1.1.msi)

2.  In powershell run `podman machine init` 

3.  and `podman machine start`

**Describe the results you received:**

```sh
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
...
API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        CreateFile \\.\pipe\docker_engine: All pipe instances are busy.
...
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```sh
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
...
API forwarding listening on: npipe:////./pipe/docker_engine
...
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Window named pipes are created according to
```sh
 [System.IO.Directory]::GetFiles("\\.\\pipe\\") | Select-String 'docker|podman'

\\.\\pipe\\docker_engine
\\.\\pipe\\podman-machine-default
```
but the podman-deamon is never started in the fedora linux distribution. 
If I understand it correctly a psuedo-systemd proccess should have been bootstrapped within fedora.
`wsl -d podman-machine-default enterns su user` does not work either because, when checking `enterns`:  

```sh
wsl -d podman-machine-default
[root@XXXXXXXXXX]# cat /usr/local/bin/enterns
#!/bin/bash
SYSDPID=`ps -eo cmd,pid | grep -m 1 ^/lib/systemd/systemd | awk '{print $2}'`
if [ ! -z "$SYSDPID" ] && [ "$SYSDPID" != "1" ]; then
        nsenter -m -p -t $SYSDPID "$@"
fi
```
no `/lib/systemd/systemd/´ is running.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
From powershell: 
podman --version
podman.exe version 4.1.1

From fedora
wsl -d podman-machine-default
[root@xxxxxxx]# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0-rc2
API Version:  4.2.0-rc2
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Built:        Wed Aug  3 16:27:27 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
Wrong version of podman 4.2.0-rc2 inside fedora as it not corresponding to v.4.1.1?

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

From fedora image using `wsl -d podman-machine-default`

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**
From fedora image using `wsl -d podman-machine-default`
```
podman-4.2.0~rc2-1.fc35.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Had an offending %USERPROFILE%/.wslconfig
```
[wsl2]

memory=8GB
kernelCommandLine=cgroup_no_v1=named
```
Removed above file and `podman machine start`  works now:
Question is if it's okey to run different version of client and server
```
podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Git Commit:   f73d8f8875c2be7cd2049094c29aff90b1150241
Built:        Wed Jun 15 15:17:12 2022
OS/Arch:      windows/amd64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0-rc2
API Version:  4.2.0-rc2
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Built:        Wed Aug  3 16:27:27 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Hello, I had exactly the same problem! But I don't have any %USERPROFILE%/.wslconfig.

Did somebody have an issue?

> Hello, I had exactly the same problem! But I don't have any %USERPROFILE%/.wslconfig.
> 
> Did somebody have an issue?

Resolved it by stopping the machine, rm'ing the machine, and then run podman init followed by podman machine start.

EDIT: Warning, you will lose all your containers and images.

> > Hello, I had exactly the same problem! But I don't have any %USERPROFILE%/.wslconfig.
> > Did somebody have an issue?
> 
> Resolved it by stopping the machine, rm'ing the machine, and then run podman init followed by podman machine start.
> 
> EDIT: Warning, you will lose all your containers and images.

Yes, we are doing this but that's not the target to lose each time containers. So I wanted to know if there is another solution. Because if I understand the error, maybe if the machine is not properly stop, it continue to listen on npipe:////./pipe/docker_engine.

> > > Hello, I had exactly the same problem! But I don't have any %USERPROFILE%/.wslconfig.
> > > Did somebody have an issue?
> > 
> > 
> > Resolved it by stopping the machine, rm'ing the machine, and then run podman init followed by podman machine start.
> > EDIT: Warning, you will lose all your containers and images.
> 
> Yes, we are doing this but that's not the target to lose each time containers. So I wanted to know if there is another solution. Because if I understand the error, maybe if the machine is not properly stop, it continue to listen on npipe:////./pipe/docker_engine.

I think my vpn was causing this error. Always starting the podman machine before the vpn solved it for me.

Hope this helps.

> I think my vpn was causing this error. Always starting the podman machine before the vpn solved it for me.
> 
> Hope this helps.

I can confirm: the VPN caused the problem for me too. You have to start the podman machine before establishing the VPN connection.

Got the same problem, resolved by

`podman machine stop`
`wsl --shutdown`

If still issue persists, reboot system after running above commands.
